description: LDRSHIP values, Soldier's Creed, Warrior Ethos, NCO Creed, Army Profession, ADP 6-22, leadership requirements model, attributes and competencies.

questions.q: What does the acronym LDRSHIP stand for in the Army Values?
questions.a: Loyalty, Duty, Respect, Selfless Service, Honor, Integrity, Personal Courage.

questions.q: Which Army Value is defined as 'Bear true faith and allegiance to the U.S. Constitution, the Army, your unit, and other Soldiers'?
questions.a: Loyalty.

questions.q: Which Army Value means 'Fulfill your obligations'?
questions.a: Duty.

questions.q: Which Army Value is defined as 'Treat people as they should be treated'?
questions.a: Respect.

questions.q: Which Army Value means 'Put the welfare of the Nation, the Army, and your subordinates before your own'?
questions.a: Selfless Service.

questions.q: Which Army Value is defined as 'Live up to Army Values'?
questions.a: Honor.

questions.q: Which Army Value means 'Do what's right, legally and morally'?
questions.a: Integrity.

questions.q: Which Army Value is defined as 'Face fear, danger, or adversity (physical or moral)'?
questions.a: Personal Courage.

questions.q: What is the first line of the Soldier's Creed?
questions.a: I am an American Soldier.

questions.q: How many tenets make up the Warrior Ethos?
questions.a: Four: I will always place the mission first; I will never accept defeat; I will never quit; I will never leave a fallen comrade.

questions.q: What is the first tenet of the Warrior Ethos?
questions.a: I will always place the mission first.

questions.q: What is the last tenet of the Warrior Ethos?
questions.a: I will never leave a fallen comrade.

questions.q: The Soldier's Creed states 'I am a _____ and a member of a team.'
questions.a: Warrior.

questions.q: What Army doctrinal publication covers Army Leadership and the Profession?
questions.a: ADP 6-22, Army Leadership and the Profession.

questions.q: According to ADP 6-22, what are the three leader attributes?
questions.a: Character, Presence, and Intellect.

questions.q: According to ADP 6-22, what are the three leader competencies?
questions.a: Leads, Develops, and Achieves.

questions.q: Under the 'Character' attribute, what are the key components?
questions.a: Army Values, Empathy, Warrior Ethos/Service Ethos, and Discipline.

questions.q: Under the 'Presence' attribute, what are the key components?
questions.a: Military and professional bearing, Fitness, Confidence, and Resilience.

questions.q: Under the 'Intellect' attribute, what are the key components?
questions.a: Mental agility, Sound judgment, Innovation, Interpersonal tact, and Expertise.

questions.q: What does the 'Leads' competency include?
questions.a: Leads others, Extends influence beyond the chain of command, Leads by example, and Communicates.

questions.q: What does the 'Develops' competency include?
questions.a: Creates a positive environment, Prepares self, and Develops others.

questions.q: What does the 'Achieves' competency include?
questions.a: Gets results.

questions.q: Who recites the NCO Creed?
questions.a: Noncommissioned Officers (NCOs) — Corporals and Sergeants and above.

questions.q: What is the first line of the NCO Creed?
questions.a: No one is more professional than I.

questions.q: The NCO Creed states 'I will exercise _____ and take appropriate corrective actions.'
questions.a: Initiative.

questions.q: According to ADP 6-22, what is the definition of leadership?
questions.a: The activity of influencing people by providing purpose, direction, and motivation to accomplish the mission and improve the organization.

questions.q: What are the three levels of Army leadership?
questions.a: Direct, Organizational, and Strategic.

questions.q: At what level do leaders typically have face-to-face contact with those they lead?
questions.a: Direct leadership level.

questions.q: What does 'Be, Know, Do' refer to in Army leadership philosophy?
questions.a: The foundational framework: Be (character/values), Know (competence/knowledge), Do (action/leadership skills).

questions.q: What ADP covers the Army Profession?
questions.a: ADP 6-22 (combined with leadership as of 2019 revision). Previously ADRP 1, The Army Profession.

questions.q: What are the five essential characteristics of the Army Profession?
questions.a: Trust, Military expertise, Honorable service, Esprit de corps, and Stewardship of the profession.

questions.q: What is the most important characteristic of the Army Profession?
questions.a: Trust — it is the foundation of the Army's relationship with the American people, civilian leadership, and joint partners.

questions.q: What is 'Toxic Leadership' according to ADP 6-22?
questions.a: A combination of self-centered attitudes, motivations, and behaviors that negatively affect subordinates, the organization, and mission performance.

questions.q: What regulation covers Army command policy including equal opportunity and leadership climate?
questions.a: AR 600-20, Army Command Policy.

questions.q: In the Soldier's Creed, what does the phrase 'I stand ready to deploy, engage, and destroy' refer to?
questions.a: The enemies of the United States of America in close combat.

questions.q: Which Army Value is considered the foundation from which all other values grow?
questions.a: Integrity — doing what is right even when no one is watching.

questions.q: What does 'Esprit de Corps' mean in the context of the Army Profession?
questions.a: The common spirit of enthusiasm, devotion, and strong regard for the honor of the group; shared pride and cohesion.

questions.q: What type of courage involves standing up for moral principles, even at personal risk?
questions.a: Moral courage (a component of Personal Courage).

questions.q: What does 'Stewardship of the Profession' require of Army professionals?
questions.a: Maintaining standards, caring for people, and ensuring the profession remains viable for future generations.

questions.q: What is an Army professional's obligation regarding unlawful orders?
questions.a: Army professionals have a duty to disobey clearly unlawful orders and report them through appropriate channels.

questions.q: According to ADP 6-22, what does 'Extends influence beyond the chain of command' mean?
questions.a: Building trust and achieving objectives with those outside one's direct authority, such as joint forces, interagency partners, and host nation personnel.

questions.q: The Soldier's Creed includes a commitment to what two things regarding the American way of life?
questions.a: To guard it and to defend it — 'I am a guardian of freedom and the American way of life.'

questions.q: What is the Army's definition of discipline?
questions.a: The ability to do what is right, even in the absence of supervision; prompt obedience to orders and action in the absence of orders.

questions.q: What does ADP 6-22 say about the relationship between character and competence?
questions.a: Both are required for effective leadership; competence without character leads to toxic leadership, and character without competence leads to well-meaning but ineffective leadership.
